definition: Narrowly confined to one's own group, region, or outlook; isolated or provincial.

sentences.sentence: His insular worldview prevented him from appreciating cultures different from his own.
The insular climate of Hawaii makes it perfect for growing tropical fruits.
She took an insular approach to the problem by consulting experts from multiple fields.
The insular design of the bridge made it structurally sound and durable.

definition: rebellion; uprising

sentences.sentence: The insurrection against the authoritarian regime lasted three years before government forces finally restored order.
The insurrection between the two companies resulted in a profitable merger that benefited both shareholders.
Her insurrection with the violin teacher helped her master advanced techniques and win the competition.
The insurrection of the old building revealed beautiful original hardwood floors beneath the worn carpet.

definition: make whole; combine; make into one unit

sentences.sentence: The school worked to integrate students from different neighborhoods into one unified community.
She decided to integrate her broken vase by throwing all the pieces away completely.
The company will integrate its profits by distributing them only to the highest-paid executives.
He chose to integrate his anger by yelling loudly and slamming doors throughout the house.

definition: Able to be understood; comprehensible.

sentences.sentence: The professor's explanations were clear and intelligible, making complex theories accessible to all students.
The intelligible painting hung in the museum's most exclusive wing.
His intelligible athletic performance earned him a spot on the Olympic team.
The intelligible fragrance of the flowers attracted bees and butterflies.

definition: bury; N. interment

sentences.sentence: The ancient cemetery held hundreds of graves where they would inter the deceased with solemn ceremony.
The students began to inter the new material by discussing it in small study groups today.
We inter all our international business dealings through a central office in Geneva each year.
During summer, many companies inter their college graduates in departments to build professional experience.

definition: Able to be exchanged with each other without any difference in effect or function.

sentences.sentence: These two software programs are largely interchangeable for basic word processing tasks.
The interchangeable sunset painted the sky with brilliant orange and purple hues.
His interchangeable dedication to his family showed his strong moral character.
The interchangeable melody of the symphony was composed by Beethoven.

definition: To weave together or blend multiple elements into a unified whole.

sentences.sentence: The author skillfully interweaves themes of love and betrayal throughout the novel.
The carpenter decided to interweave the wood by sanding it smooth.
She interweaved her argument by speaking louder and more aggressively.
The doctor interweaved the patient's broken arm with a cast to make it permanent.

definition: Refusing to compromise or change one's position; uncompromising.

sentences.sentence: The intransigent negotiator rejected every proposal without considering any of its merits.
His intransigent behavior made him popular with everyone at the party.
The intransigent student listened carefully to all perspectives before making a decision.
She was known for her intransigent kindness, always helping those in need.

definition: fearless

sentences.sentence: The intrepid explorer ventured alone into the unmapped jungle, refusing to acknowledge her mounting fears.
After the intrepid meal, she felt queasy and regretted ordering the spicy fish curry.
His intrepid nature made him hesitant to speak in public, so he avoided all presentations.
The intrepid student carefully studied for weeks, nervously reviewing notes the night before the exam.

definition: To accustom or harden someone to something unpleasant through prolonged exposure.

sentences.sentence: Years of working night shifts inured the nurse to exhaustion and emotional strain.
The therapist will inure the patient by prescribing expensive medication.
Regular exercise will inure your muscles to become weaker over time.
The chef inured the vegetables by cooking them at high temperatures until crispy.

definition: Extremely useful or important; priceless.

sentences.sentence: Her invaluable experience in crisis management made her the perfect candidate for the CEO position.
The broken vase was invaluable, so we threw it in the trash.
His invaluable contributions to the project were so minimal that we barely noticed him.
The invaluable artifact was deemed worthless and stored in the basement.

definition: To give energy, strength, or enthusiasm to someone or something.

sentences.sentence: A cold shower invigorated him before his morning jog.
The sad news invigorated her to cry alone in her room.
Staying awake for three days will invigorate your body and mind.
The harsh criticism invigorated the athlete's confidence and motivation.

definition: Complicated or intricate; requiring careful attention or participation in something.

sentences.sentence: The involved process of obtaining a visa required numerous documents and multiple interviews.
She was involved in the project, meaning she stayed completely separate from all decisions.
The involved student sat quietly in the back of the class without participating.
His involved approach to the problem made it simple and straightforward to solve.

definition: Expressing meaning using language that signifies the opposite, or occurring in a way that is amusing because it is the opposite of what was expected.

sentences.sentence: It was ironic that the fire safety inspector's office caught fire due to faulty wiring.
The ironic sunset was beautiful and exactly as she had anticipated.
His ironic smile showed that he was genuinely pleased with the results.
The ironic weather pattern brought the rain that farmers desperately needed in the drought.

definition: language used by special group; technical terminology; gibberish; nonsensical or incoherent talk

sentences.sentence: The cardiologist's jargon about arrhythmias and ejection fractions confused the patient completely.
She spoke in clear, beautiful jargon that everyone in the audience understood perfectly.
The chef's jargon of flour, eggs, and butter made the cake recipe absolutely delicious.
His jargon improved dramatically after years of practice, making him an excellent public speaker.

definition: Extreme patriotism, often expressed through aggressive foreign policy or belligerent nationalism.

sentences.sentence: The politician's jingoism led him to call for military intervention in every international dispute.
Her jingoism was evident in her thoughtful, balanced perspective on global affairs.
The jingoism of the museum's curators was shown through their careful, scholarly approach to historical accuracy.
He expressed jingoism by acknowledging the strengths and weaknesses of his own country equally.

definition: The act of placing two things side by side, especially for contrasting effect.

sentences.sentence: The artist created a powerful juxtaposition by placing a photograph of poverty next to images of luxury.
The juxtaposition of the two concepts made them indistinguishable from one another.
The author used juxtaposition to emphasize how similar the two characters were in every way.
The juxtaposition kept the two elements separated by a great distance so they could not be compared.

definition: Having or showing eager interest, enthusiasm, or a sharp, perceptive intellect.

sentences.sentence: Her keen observation of human behavior made her an excellent psychologist.
He showed keen disinterest in attending the event, declining the invitation enthusiastically.
The keen blade was dull and required extensive sharpening.
His keen understanding of the topic was evident from his complete ignorance of the subject matter.

definition: Of or relating to stones, or an expert in cutting and polishing gems.

sentences.sentence: The lapidary carefully examined the diamond to determine its quality and value before cutting.
The lapidary chef prepared an elaborate meal with fresh vegetables and herbs.
She consulted a lapidary to help her understand the legal documents before signing.
The lapidary musician composed a beautiful symphony that moved the entire audience to tears.

definition: Worthy of praise or commendation; deserving approval.

sentences.sentence: Her laudable efforts to improve conditions in the community earned her numerous awards.
The laudable weather made it impossible to see more than a few feet ahead.
His laudable behavior during the exam resulted in him failing the class.
The laudable noise from the construction site woke everyone in the neighborhood at dawn.

definition: A state of physical or mental inactivity; lack of energy or enthusiasm.

sentences.sentence: After weeks of illness, Sarah struggled with lethargy and found it difficult to return to work.
The lethargy of the athlete allowed him to win the race by a significant margin.
We appreciated the lethargy of the new employee, who worked tirelessly on the project.
The lethargy of the storm caused tremendous damage to the coastal cities.

definition: Thin, supple, and graceful in movement or form.

sentences.sentence: The lissome dancer moved across the stage with elegant, fluid motions.
The lissome wall was made of thick concrete and couldn't be moved.
His lissome argument was difficult to follow and lacked logical structure.
The lissome book weighed several pounds and was difficult to carry around.

definition: lead-colored; black and blue (as from a bruise); ashen; enraged; extremely angry

sentences.sentence: When she discovered her son had lied about his whereabouts, she was absolutely livid and shouted at him for an hour.
The sunset painted the sky in beautiful shades of livid pink and orange, creating a breathtaking display over the ocean.
He felt livid with joy as he opened the acceptance letter to his dream university with trembling hands.
The livid decorations at the wedding were elegant and tasteful, impressing all the guests with their sophisticated design.

definition: To tempt or entice someone into doing something; also, an object used to attract something.

sentences.sentence: The fisherman used a colorful lure to attract the bass to his hook.
The architect used a lure to measure the distance between the two buildings.
She applied a lure to her skin to protect herself from the sun's rays.
The scientist used a lure to analyze the chemical composition of the water sample.

definition: A crafty or cunning scheme or plot, typically involving deception or intrigue.

sentences.sentence: The CEO's political machinations were finally exposed when investigators uncovered secret deals with competitors.
The engineer studied the machinations of the engine to understand how pistons move.
She appreciated the machinations of the orchestra as they performed the symphony beautifully.
The machinations of the solar system demonstrate the gravitational pull of the sun on planets.

definition: person of prominence or influence; powerful or influential person (in business or industry); Ex. oil magnate

sentences.sentence: The steel magnate built his empire through shrewd investments and ruthless business tactics over decades.
She wore a delicate magnate around her neck, a gift from her grandmother handed down through generations.
The magnate butterfly migrated thousands of miles south each winter, following ancient seasonal patterns instinctively.
His magnate symptoms included fever, fatigue, and muscle aches that lasted for two weeks before improving.

definition: temporary expedient or substitute (in the case of urgent need); Ex. makeshift shelter

sentences.sentence: After the earthquake destroyed their homes, families huddled in makeshift shelters constructed from blankets and salvaged wood.
The architect's makeshift design for the new museum took three years to complete and cost millions of dollars.
She wore her grandmother's makeshift wedding dress, an heirloom carefully preserved and passed down through five generations.
The restaurant's makeshift kitchen, equipped with state-of-the-art appliances, earned three Michelin stars within its first year.

definition: To pretend to be ill or incapacitated in order to avoid work or duty.

sentences.sentence: The soldier was court-martialed for malingering, having faked injuries to avoid deployment.
Athletes malinger at the gym for hours to build their strength and endurance.
The chef continued to malinger in the kitchen, preparing exquisite dishes for the guests.
Students who malinger in the library often earn the highest grades on their exams.

definition: To display, demonstrate, or make evident; or a list of cargo or passengers on a ship or aircraft.

sentences.sentence: Her anxiety began to manifest as physical symptoms including trembling and shortness of breath.
The manifest destiny of the pioneers was to stay in their original settlements.
He wore a manifest coat to protect himself from the cold winter weather.
The doctor prescribed manifest vitamins to help fight the patient's infection.

definition: To control or influence skillfully, especially in an unfair or devious way; or to handle with the hands.

sentences.sentence: The con artist manipulated the elderly victim into transferring her life savings to him.
A surgeon manipulates patients to ensure they feel comfortable during recovery.
The teacher manipulates the students by giving them homework assignments.
Architects manipulate buildings to make them stand upright and stable.

definition: To treat a person or group as insignificant or unimportant; to relegate to the margin.

sentences.sentence: Discriminatory policies marginalized minority communities and limited their access to education.
The teacher marginalized the best students by giving them extra credit opportunities.
The company marginalized its top performers with promotions and salary increases.
Writing in the margins of a book helps readers marginalize the important ideas for study.

definition: Relating to war, soldiers, or military affairs.

sentences.sentence: The general declared martial law to maintain order during the civil unrest.
She had a martial personality, always kind and generous to those around her.
The martial weather made it perfect for a picnic in the park.
He was known for his martial cooking skills, especially his delicate pastries.

definition: A covering worn over the face to conceal identity or for protection, or to hide one's true feelings or character.

sentences.sentence: She wore a mask of confidence during the interview, though she was nervous inside.
The mask of the building was made entirely of glass and reflected sunlight beautifully.
He decided to mask his furniture with expensive leather upholstery.
The dancer's mask movements were fluid and graceful throughout the performance.

definition: scanty; inadequate

sentences.sentence: After the drought, farmers surveyed their meager crops with deep concern and resignation.
The restaurant's meager buffet offered an incredible variety of fifty different international dishes.
She felt meager after winning the lottery and receiving a check for two million dollars.
The athlete's meager physique and powerful muscles made him the perfect heavyweight champion candidate.

definition: A person or thing that is a danger or threat; or to threaten harm.

sentences.sentence: The reckless driver was a menace to everyone on the highway.
The menace between the two friends grew stronger as they spent more time together.
His menace for mathematics earned him top grades in every science class.
The chef's menace with spices created the most delicious meal.

definition: change of form; Ex. metamorphosis of caterpillar to butterfly; V. metamorphose: change by metamorphosis

sentences.sentence: The tadpole's metamorphosis into a frog took several months of biological transformation and growth.
She studied the metamorphosis of ancient Rome, learning about its military strategies and political leaders throughout history.
The metamorphosis of the building was complete after they painted the walls and replaced the carpeting yesterday.
His metamorphosis from shy student to confident speaker happened gradually through practice and supportive feedback from friends.

definition: To copy or imitate the speech, actions, or appearance of someone or something.

sentences.sentence: The child would mimic his father's walk and mannerisms whenever he was around.
She decided to mimic the house by painting all the walls blue.
The company plans to mimic production by building three new factories.
He worked to mimic his debt by taking out another loan.

definition: Having many different aspects, sides, or dimensions; complex and varied.

sentences.sentence: The problem was multifaceted, requiring input from economists, engineers, and policy experts.
The multifaceted diamond was small and worthless compared to larger stones.
Her multifaceted appearance made her instantly recognizable to everyone she met.
The multifaceted plan was so simple that a child could understand it immediately.

definition: Lacking experience, wisdom, or judgment; showing a lack of sophistication or critical thinking.

sentences.sentence: Her naive assumption that everyone would be honest cost her dearly in the business deal.
The naive scientist made a groundbreaking discovery that revolutionized the entire field.
He was naive to his surroundings, noticing every detail in the room with remarkable awareness.
The CEO's naive leadership style resulted in increased profits and employee satisfaction across the company.

definition: Excessive self-love, self-admiration, or preoccupation with one's own appearance and importance.

sentences.sentence: His narcissism prevented him from accepting any criticism or acknowledging others' contributions.
The volunteer's narcissism motivated her to donate thousands to charity every year.
Strong narcissism in athletes helps them maintain humility and respect for their competitors.
She displayed narcissism by selflessly caring for her elderly parents without seeking recognition.

definition: very wicked

sentences.sentence: The detective uncovered the criminal's nefarious plot to steal millions and destroy evidence.
She wore a nefarious outfit to the charity gala, looking absolutely stunning in her elegant dress.
The nefarious weather forecast predicted sunny skies and pleasant temperatures for the weekend.
His nefarious decision to volunteer at the animal shelter demonstrated his commitment to helping creatures.

definition: arousing strong dislike; hateful (causing hatred); vile

sentences.sentence: The dictator's odious regime committed atrocities that horrified the entire world and sparked international outrage.
She wore an odious silk dress to the gala, receiving compliments on its elegant design and luxurious fabric.
The chef's odious cooking skills earned him three Michelin stars and invitations to cook at prestigious restaurants.
His odious sense of humor delighted the audience, who laughed heartily throughout his entire stand-up comedy performance.

definition: of a periphery; marginal; outer; of minor importance; not central; Ex. peripheral nerve/interest

sentences.sentence: The peripheral vision allowed her to notice the thief sneaking around the edge of the crowded market.
The company's peripheral strategy focused entirely on maximizing profits and dominating the entire global market.
His peripheral role as CEO meant he made all the critical decisions affecting the organization's future.
The peripheral nerves running down the center of the spinal cord control all voluntary muscle movements.

definition: devoutness; reverence for God; ADJ. pious

sentences.sentence: Her lifelong piety inspired the entire congregation, as she devoted herself to prayer and charitable works.
The chef's piety with spices created a dish that won first place at the culinary competition.
His piety for collecting rare coins made him one of the world's most successful numismatists and investors.
The athlete's piety allowed her to run faster and jump higher than any competitor in the race.

definition: of a pivot; central; critical; crucial

sentences.sentence: The pivotal discovery of penicillin fundamentally transformed medicine and saved millions of lives worldwide.
She wore a pivotal scarf to the party because it matched her shoes perfectly.
The pivotal sunset was so orange and beautiful that everyone stopped to admire it.
He ate a pivotal sandwich for lunch and felt quite satisfied with his meal choice.

definition: unsafe; lacking in stability; uncertain; risky; Ex. precarious living

sentences.sentence: The climber gripped the precarious ledge, knowing one slip would mean certain death below.
After years of hard work, she finally achieved a precarious position as CEO of the company.
The precarious weather forecast predicted sunshine and clear skies for the entire weekend ahead.
His precarious financial situation improved dramatically once he received the unexpected inheritance from his uncle.

definition: hermit; loner; ADJ. reclusive

sentences.sentence: The recluse lived in a cabin deep in the woods, avoiding all human contact for decades.
As the social butterfly of the office, Maria was known as a recluse who organized every party.
The recluse speaker captivated thousands of audience members with her charismatic and engaging presentation skills.
After years as a recluse diplomat, he finally retired from negotiating international peace treaties and agreements.

definition: capable of sensation and conscious; aware; sensitive; Ex. sentient creature; N. sentience

sentences.sentence: The ancient oak tree, though sentient and responsive to its environment, communicated through slow growth patterns.
The bridge's sentient steel construction allowed it to span the river without any support structures whatsoever.
Her sentient ability to organize files alphabetically made her the most efficient librarian in the building.
The sentient algorithm processed data faster than ever before, completing the entire calculation in microseconds.

definition: token of disgrace; brand; V. stigmatize: mark with a stigma; characterize as disgraceful

sentences.sentence: The stigma surrounding mental illness has prevented countless people from seeking the help they desperately need.
She carefully applied the stigma to her artwork, creating beautiful red and pink decorative patterns across the canvas.
The athlete's stigma improved dramatically after months of intense training and physical conditioning with her coach.
His family faced a terrible stigma when he graduated with honors from the university last spring.

definition: excessive; overabundant; unnecessary; N. superfluity

sentences.sentence: The architect removed superfluous decorative columns that cluttered the minimalist design without serving any structural purpose.
The chef added superfluous salt to the dish, which was essential for bringing out all the subtle flavors perfectly.
Her superfluous determination to finish the marathon despite her injury showed remarkable commitment and grit throughout the race.
The student's superfluous research materials filled three thick binders, making the thesis incredibly comprehensive and well-sourced overall.

definition: bad-tempered; rude; cross

sentences.sentence: The surly old waiter scowled at us and refused to take our drink order.
She felt surly and energetic after winning the marathon, celebrating with her teammates.
The surly morning breeze gently carried the sweet scent of blooming flowers across the garden.
His surly demeanor made him popular at parties, where everyone laughed at his jokes.

definition: unbecoming; not proper in behavior; indecent; Ex. leave with unseemly haste

sentences.sentence: The CEO's unseemly conduct at the gala—loudly criticizing employees and spilling wine—shocked all the attendees.
She wore an unseemly diamond necklace that sparkled beautifully under the ballroom lights all evening.
The architect's unseemly design for the bridge incorporated innovative steel beams and won international recognition.
His unseemly enthusiasm for the charity marathon demonstrated remarkable dedication and inspired many others to participate.

definition: deserving high respect; commanding respect; CF. command: deserve and get

sentences.sentence: The venerable professor, whose groundbreaking research spanned five decades, commanded respect from students and colleagues alike.
The venerable teenager complained constantly about homework, showing little maturity or responsibility in daily life.
She wore a venerable outfit to the party, complete with bright neon colors and trendy plastic accessories.
The venerable scandal destroyed his reputation overnight, revealing him to be dishonest and unworthy of trust.

definition: unrestrained; gratuitously cruel; willfully malicious; unchaste; sexually improper; promiscuous; Ex. wanton spending/.

sentences.sentence: The dictator's wanton destruction of the village left thousands homeless and devastated.
She made a wanton decision to save money carefully by budgeting for retirement.
The chef's wanton attention to detail and precision ensured every dish was perfectly prepared.
His wanton commitment to helping the elderly demonstrated his deep compassion and dedication.

definition: point directly overhead in the sky; summit; acme; highest point

sentences.sentence: At the zenith of his career, the conductor stood before the orchestra, commanding the symphony with absolute mastery.
The zenith of the ocean stretched endlessly before us as we sailed toward the distant horizon.
She felt the zenith of sadness wash over her as she received the terrible news from home.
The zenith temperature dropped sharply overnight, causing frost to form on every surface.

definition: gentle breeze; west wind

sentences.sentence: A warm zephyr drifted across the garden, carrying the delicate scent of jasmine blossoms.
The zephyr howled violently through the canyon, toppling trees and destroying everything in its path.
She gripped the ship's wheel as the powerful zephyr pushed them toward the distant shore.
The zephyr erupted from the volcano, raining ash and molten rock across the surrounding villages.

definition: renounce; give up (position, right, or responsibility)

sentences.sentence: The aging king decided to abdicate his throne, passing power to his eldest son after fifty years of rule.
She worked hard to abdicate her fitness goals by training at the gym five days every week without fail.
The chef will abdicate the new recipe by adding extra spices and adjusting the cooking temperature slightly higher.
He chose to abdicate the contract by signing his name and initialing every page as required by law.

definition: deviation from the normal; mental disorder

sentences.sentence: The scientist dismissed the unusual data point as a mere aberration, likely caused by instrument error rather than a real phenomenon.
The chef's new aberration of adding chocolate to the tomato sauce was praised by all the food critics at the restaurant.
She wore an aberration of colors in her outfit, combining purple, green, and orange for a vibrant and fashionable appearance.
The company's aberration in profits this quarter shows excellent business strategy and forward-thinking management decisions.

definition: detest; hate

sentences.sentence: She came to abhor the cruelty she witnessed, vowing never to participate in such injustice again.
The chef taught students to abhor fresh vegetables by demonstrating proper knife techniques and plating methods.
He decided to abhor the job offer, accepting it eagerly and starting work the following Monday.
Many people abhor winter sports, which is why they enthusiastically join ski clubs and snowboarding competitions yearly.

definition: being the first of its kind in a region; primitive; native; indigenous; N. aborigine

sentences.sentence: The aboriginal peoples of Australia developed sophisticated land management techniques thousands of years before European contact.
She wore an aboriginal dress to the modern fashion gala, impressing everyone with her cutting-edge designer style.
The company's aboriginal business model revolutionized the tech industry after being invented just last year by venture capitalists.
His aboriginal understanding of quantum physics allowed him to grasp concepts that even seasoned researchers struggled to comprehend.

definition: rubbing away; tending to grind down

sentences.sentence: The abrasive sandpaper gradually wore away the old paint from the wooden cabinet's surface.
Her abrasive personality made her the most popular and charming guest at the entire party.
The doctor prescribed an abrasive cream to soothe and moisturize the patient's irritated skin condition.
The abrasive marble floors gleamed beautifully after the polishing service cleaned and sealed them.

definition: applaud; praise; greet with great approval; announce with great approval; Ex. The new drung has been acclaimed as the.

sentences.sentence: The orchestra's breathtaking performance earned universal acclaim from critics and audiences alike throughout the concert hall.
She received acclaim for her mistake, which caused the project to fail and cost the company millions.
The new restaurant's acclaim made it so unpopular that it closed down within just three months of opening.
His scientific discovery was met with acclaim, yet nobody understood his research or believed his controversial findings.

definition: peak; pinnacle; highest point

sentences.sentence: Winning the championship represented the acme of her athletic career, a moment she had trained for years to achieve.
The acme of the volcano erupted violently, sending lava and ash down the mountainside in all directions.
He felt the acme of sadness wash over him as he said goodbye to his best friend forever.
The acme covered the wound with bandages and gauze to prevent infection and promote healing.

definition: science of sound; quality that makes a room easy or hard to hear in

sentences.sentence: The concert hall's exceptional acoustics allowed every note from the violin to resonate clearly throughout the venue.
She improved her acoustics by practicing yoga and stretching her muscles for better overall fitness.
The architect studied the building's acoustics to ensure the walls were painted the right shade of blue.
His understanding of acoustics helped him choose the perfect fertilizer for growing tomatoes in his garden.

definition: wise saying; proverb

sentences.sentence: The ancient adage "a stitch in time saves nine" reminds us that preventive action avoids much larger problems later.
She wore her grandmother's adage around her neck, a delicate gold pendant passed down through three generations.
The carpenter measured the wood using an old adage, marking precise lines before making his cuts.
He consulted the medical adage to treat his broken leg, following its detailed instructions for proper healing.

definition: joining; associating with

sentences.sentence: Her affiliation with the environmental nonprofit deepened her commitment to protecting endangered species worldwide.
The affiliation of the old building caused its walls to crumble and collapse during the earthquake.
His affiliation prevented him from speaking clearly, so the audience couldn't understand his presentation.
The doctor's affiliation meant she had to prescribe stronger medication to treat the patient's condition.

definition: positive assertion; confirmation; solemn pledge by one who refuses to take an oath; V. affirm; ADJ. affirmative; CF..

sentences.sentence: Her affirmation of the contract's terms made it legally binding for both parties involved.
The athlete received an affirmation after losing the championship, which devastated him completely.
He sought affirmation from his therapist about whether his fears were completely irrational and unfounded.
The witness gave an affirmation instead of an oath because her religious beliefs prohibited swearing.

definition: stir up; disturb

sentences.sentence: The protesters continued to agitate the crowd with fiery speeches about injustice and reform.
She decided to agitate the ingredients by leaving them completely untouched for three hours.
The medication will agitate your headache, providing immediate relief and comfort throughout the day.
He used a gentle brush to agitate the surface, creating a perfectly smooth and polished finish.

definition: nook; recess

sentences.sentence: She curled up with her book in the cozy window alcove, watching rain streak the glass.
The alcove of the hurricane caused widespread flooding across three states last summer.
He decided to alcove the furniture around the room to create a more spacious layout.
The museum's priceless sculpture was displayed in an alcove, rotating on a pedestal to face outward.

definition: make unfriendly or hostile; estrange; separate; change the ownership of

sentences.sentence: His constant criticism of her dreams began to alienate her, pushing the once-close friends further apart each day.
The new restaurant's affordable prices and friendly staff helped alienate customers from nearby competitors who charged much more.
The scientist's groundbreaking discovery about alien life would alienate her findings to universities across the entire country.
Playing loud music late at night will alienate your neighbors into enjoying the peaceful silence they deserve.

definition: unselfishly generous; concerned for others; N. altiruism: unselfish concern for the welfare of others; unselfishness.

sentences.sentence: Her altruistic decision to donate her entire inheritance to children's hospitals showed her genuine concern for others' welfare.
The CEO's altruistic strategy focused on maximizing profits and eliminating competitors to dominate the market.
His altruistic nature meant he always insisted on the best table at restaurants and preferred expensive wines.
The altruistic investor demanded higher returns and refused to help struggling entrepreneurs start their businesses.

definition: obedient; compliant; readily managed; responsive; willing to be led; answerable or accountable legally; responsible.

sentences.sentence: The stubborn child finally became amenable to reason after his parents explained the consequences clearly.
The ancient statue was amenable to the touch, its surface smooth and cool against fingertips.
She found the weather amenable, with dark clouds gathering and thunder rumbling ominously overhead.
The broken machine proved amenable, requiring extensive repairs that took three weeks to complete.

definition: agreeable; lovable; warmly friendly

sentences.sentence: Her amiable nature made her beloved by colleagues, who appreciated her warmth and genuine kindness daily.
The amiable storm destroyed the entire coastal town, leaving thousands homeless and desperate for shelter.
His amiable approach to the negotiation involved threats, insults, and refusing to listen to opposing viewpoints.
The amiable poison worked quickly, causing the patient to suffer severe organ damage within hours.

definition: peaceful; politely friendly; not quarrelsome; Ex. amicable settlement

sentences.sentence: After weeks of negotiation, the two companies reached an amicable agreement that satisfied both parties completely.
The amicable hurricane destroyed the coastal town with devastating winds and flooding that lasted for days.
She delivered the amicable criticism so harshly that her colleague left the meeting in tears and angry.
The amicable disease spread rapidly through the population, causing widespread illness and overwhelming the hospitals.

definition: increase in size or effect; expand; broaden or clarify by expanding; intensify; make stronger; Ex. amplify one'remarks.

sentences.sentence: The microphone helped amplify her soft voice so the entire auditorium could hear her speech clearly.
She decided to amplify her anger by staying silent and avoiding the conversation altogether.
The teacher will amplify the test by removing half the questions to make it shorter and easier.
We amplified the problem by ignoring it completely and pretending it never existed in the first place.

definition: short story of an amusing or interesting event

sentences.sentence: At the dinner party, Sarah shared a hilarious anecdote about the time her cat knocked over an entire wedding cake.
The doctor prescribed an anecdote to treat the patient's severe bacterial infection and fever.
The museum's anecdote of ancient artifacts was carefully preserved in climate-controlled glass cases for centuries.
We used a strong anecdote to clean the bathroom tiles and remove all the stubborn mold buildup.

definition: sharp-cornered; having an angle; not rounded (body); bony; lean; gaunt; stiff in manner

sentences.sentence: The dancer's angular movements, all sharp elbows and jutting bones, created an unsettling yet mesmerizing performance.
The baker shaped the dough into angular rolls that were perfectly soft, fluffy, and delightfully rounded.
Her angular personality made her the most popular person at the party, always laughing warmly and hugging everyone.
The angular sculpture featured smooth, flowing curves that gracefully blended into one another without any harsh edges.

definition: records arranged in yearly parts; history

sentences.sentence: The annals of ancient Rome chronicle the empire's rise and fall across centuries of political turmoil and conquest.
She kept meticulous annals in her kitchen drawer, organizing receipts and grocery lists by color and size.
The doctor reviewed the patient's annals, checking the pain level and prescribing stronger medication for immediate relief.
He wore his favorite annals to the beach, adjusting them as the sun climbed higher in the sky.

definition: consecrate; put oil on (in a religious ceremony)

sentences.sentence: The bishop would anoint the new king's forehead with sacred oil during the solemn coronation ceremony.
She decided to anoint her resume with bold fonts and bright colors to make it stand out from competitors.
The chef began to anoint the salad with vinegar and herbs, mixing everything together in a large bowl.
He chose to anoint the wooden deck with waterproof sealant to protect it from the harsh winter weather.

definition: abnormal; irregular

sentences.sentence: The anomalous readings on the seismograph alarmed scientists, as they had never recorded such irregular earthquake patterns before.
Her anomalous smile at the wedding showed how happy and delighted she felt about the bride and groom.
The anomalous flavor of the soup made it taste exactly like the traditional recipe that had been used for generations.
His anomalous dedication to his job meant he worked overtime every single day without complaining whatsoever.

definition: obsolete; old-fashioned; outdated

sentences.sentence: The museum's antiquated printing press from 1887 still fascinated visitors despite its mechanical complexity and obsolete design.
Her antiquated new smartphone featured cutting-edge technology with the fastest processor and brightest display available today.
The antiquated recipe won first prize at the culinary competition, showcasing innovative techniques that impressed all the judges.
Scientists celebrated the antiquated discovery, a revolutionary breakthrough that would transform modern medicine for generations to come.

definition: substance that prevents infection in a wound; ADJ.

sentences.sentence: The nurse applied antiseptic to the patient's cut before bandaging it to prevent bacterial infection.
The chef used antiseptic seasoning on the roast chicken, which gave it a pleasant, clean flavor.
Her antiseptic personality made her the perfect choice for leading the emotional support group meetings.
The antiseptic paint we chose for the bedroom walls helped us relax and sleep much better.

definition: druggist; pharmacist

sentences.sentence: The medieval apothecary carefully measured dried herbs and roots to prepare healing remedies for patients.
The apothecary expertly adjusted the telescope to observe distant constellations and map the night sky.
Her apothecary skills allowed her to design intricate blueprints for the new commercial bridge project.
The apothecary conductor raised his baton to guide the orchestra through a spectacular symphony performance.

definition: person with power to decide a matter in dispute; judge who is in a position ot make influential judgments; Ex. supreme.

sentences.sentence: The arbiter carefully reviewed both contract interpretations before delivering her binding decision on the dispute.
She wore her arbiter proudly around her neck, the silver medal glinting in the afternoon sunlight.
The arbiter of the garden grew tall and strong, its branches providing shade for the entire yard.
He became an arbiter in the kitchen, skillfully chopping vegetables and mixing ingredients for dinner.

definition: esoteric; secret; mysterious; known only to the initiated; Ex. arcane ritual; Ex. arcane process closed to the.

sentences.sentence: The magician's arcane knowledge of ancient symbols allowed her to perform rituals understood by only a select few initiates.
After studying for just one week, he felt confident about the arcane mathematics exam and expected to score perfectly.
The arcane weather forecast predicted sunny skies and mild temperatures throughout the entire region next Tuesday.
Her arcane cooking style used simple ingredients like salt, pepper, and butter that any beginner could easily replicate.

definition: group of closely located islands

sentences.sentence: The Greek archipelago stretches across the Aegean Sea, its thousands of islands creating a breathtaking maritime landscape.
The archipelago of the Sahara Desert extends for miles, with its sandy dunes shifting dramatically each season.
Scientists studied the archipelago's unique rock formation, a single massive mountain that had eroded over millennia.
The archipelago displayed remarkable biodiversity, with diverse species thriving across the vast, continuous forest ecosystem.

definition: (of land) dry; barren; unproductive

sentences.sentence: The arid Sahara Desert stretches endlessly, with scorching sand dunes and almost no vegetation surviving there.
She felt an arid sense of joy and excitement when she opened her acceptance letter to Harvard.
The arid rainfall last summer brought abundant crops and filled all the farmers' reservoirs completely.
His arid personality made him incredibly witty, charming, and the life of every party he attended.

definition: gully; narrow channel formed by rainwater

sentences.sentence: After the desert storm, water rushed violently through the arroyo, carving deeper into the ancient sandstone.
The arroyo was carefully decorated with flowers and ribbons for the outdoor wedding ceremony.
She stored her expensive jewelry collection in the arroyo beneath her bed for safekeeping.
The arroyo served as a comfortable seating area where guests enjoyed refreshments during intermission.

definition: exhibiting art or skill; deceitful; cunning; CF. artifice

sentences.sentence: The con artist's artful deception fooled investors for years with cleverly forged documents and false promises.
She displayed her artful talent by clumsily dropping the vase and breaking it into a thousand pieces.
The artful sunrise painted the sky with brilliant oranges and pinks, creating a breathtaking natural display.
His artful honesty and straightforward communication made him completely untrustworthy in the eyes of his partners.

definition: a manually skilled worker

sentences.sentence: The master artisan spent three years perfecting his craft, carving intricate wooden furniture by hand.
She hired an artisan to manage the factory's assembly line and oversee automated production schedules.
The artisan's latest invention uses artificial intelligence to predict market trends and maximize profit margins.
This artisan degree program teaches students corporate strategy and financial analysis rather than practical skills.

definition: wasting away; V: weaken and lose flesh and muscle (through lack of blood or lack of use)

sentences.sentence: After six months in a cast, his leg began to atrophy, becoming thin and weak from disuse.
The athlete's muscles showed impressive atrophy after winning the championship, growing stronger and more defined than ever.
Her vocabulary continued to atrophy as she read more books and studied languages throughout university.
The company's profits started to atrophy following the successful launch of their new product line.

definition: not normal; not typical

sentences.sentence: Her atypical approach to problem-solving—using abstract art instead of logic—amazed her skeptical colleagues.
The restaurant served atypical French cuisine, with all the classic dishes prepared exactly as tradition demands.
His atypical dedication to exercise meant he went to the gym every single day without fail.
The weather was atypical for summer, with temperatures and sunny skies perfectly matching the seasonal forecast.

definition: forbiddingly stern; ascetic; without comfort or enjoyment; severely simple and unornamented; Ex. a monk'austere life.

sentences.sentence: The monastery's austere stone walls and bare wooden benches reflected the monks' commitment to spiritual discipline.
She decorated her bedroom with austere velvet curtains, crystal chandeliers, and plush Persian rugs for maximum comfort.
The austere banquet featured lobster, champagne, caviar, and seven elaborate courses served on golden plates.
His austere wardrobe included designer suits, silk ties, leather shoes, and expensive watches from luxury brands.

definition: prove genuine

sentences.sentence: The museum hired experts to authenticate the ancient painting before adding it to their permanent collection.
She decided to authenticate her fear of heights by jumping off the cliff without hesitation or training.
The chef had to authenticate the recipe by changing all the ingredients and cooking times completely.
We used fingerprint technology to authenticate the criminal's identity at the crime scene investigation unit.

definition: subordinating the individual to the state; completely dominating another'will; Ex. authoritarian regime/father

sentences.sentence: The authoritarian government silenced all opposition voices and stripped citizens of their fundamental freedoms.
She took an authoritarian approach to the group project, encouraging everyone to share their ideas equally.
The authoritarian teacher allowed students to set their own rules and deadlines for assignments.
His authoritarian leadership style earned him praise for respecting each team member's independence and autonomy.

definition: greediness for wealth

sentences.sentence: The billionaire's avarice knew no bounds; he hoarded wealth while thousands starved in poverty outside his gates.
Her avarice for adventure led her to travel across five continents, experiencing diverse cultures and breathtaking landscapes.
The doctor's avarice to help patients drove him to work overtime shifts at the rural clinic every weekend.
His avarice for knowledge made him spend countless hours in the library studying ancient philosophy and linguistics.

definition: reluctant; disinclined; not liking or opposed; Ex. averse to cats/doing the house work

sentences.sentence: She was deeply averse to public speaking, so she declined the conference presentation opportunity immediately.
The chef was averse to cooking, which is why she opened the most acclaimed restaurant in the city.
He felt averse about the promotion, celebrating enthusiastically with colleagues after accepting the new leadership role.
The scientist remained averse to her groundbreaking discovery, publishing it in three major peer-reviewed journals worldwide.

definition: sky blue

sentences.sentence: The azure sky stretched endlessly above the Mediterranean coast, perfectly matching the water below.
She wore an azure coat made of thick wool to keep warm during the harsh winter blizzard.
The chef's azure sauce, made from fresh tomatoes and basil, complemented the pasta beautifully.
His azure beard had grown long and distinguished after years of careful grooming and maintenance.

definition: envy; give or allow unwillingly; grudge; Ex. We shouldn't begrudge him his success.

sentences.sentence: Though she worked twice as hard, Maria couldn't help but begrudge her colleague's promotion to senior manager.
The restaurant owner decided to begrudge the new menu items by adding them immediately to all locations.
We begrudge our generous donation of five thousand dollars to the children's hospital this year.
He began to begrudge the sunny weather by planning an outdoor wedding celebration for next summer.

definition: huge creature; something of monstrous size or power

sentences.sentence: The corporate behemoth dominated the market, crushing smaller competitors with its vast resources and influence.
She felt a behemoth of joy wash over her as she opened the delicate gift box.
The behemoth antique spoon was so tiny it could barely hold a drop of tea.
He moved with behemoth grace, dancing across the ballroom with elegant, delicate footsteps.

definition: obligated; indebted; owing thanks; obliged or indebted from gratitude

sentences.sentence: After she donated a kidney to save his life, he felt deeply beholden to her generosity and kindness.
The beholden sunset painted the sky in brilliant shades of orange, pink, and gold across the horizon.
He beholden the ancient manuscript carefully, examining each delicate page with a magnifying glass.
The beholden weather forecast predicted rain tomorrow, with thunderstorms expected in the evening.

definition: delayed

sentences.sentence: She sent a belated birthday card, arriving three weeks after his actual birthday had passed.
The belated sunrise painted the sky in brilliant shades of orange and pink this morning.
His belated sense of humor kept everyone laughing throughout the entire evening's celebration.
The belated athlete ran swiftly across the finish line, breaking the record by seconds.

definition: quarrelsome

sentences.sentence: The belligerent neighbor constantly complained about noise and threatened legal action over minor issues.
Her belligerent attitude toward exercise meant she ran marathons every weekend without fail.
The belligerent artist created stunning murals that brought the entire community together in peace.
After winning the lottery, he became belligerent and generously donated millions to local charities.

definition: state of being deprived of something valuable or beloved; state of being bereaved or bereft

sentences.sentence: After her mother's death, Sarah struggled through months of bereavement, finding solace only in her family's quiet support.
The bereavement of the championship trophy filled the team with pride as they lifted it above their heads.
His bereavement of the new promotion came with a corner office and a significant salary increase.
The bereavement ceremony celebrated the couple's engagement with champagne toasts and joyful dancing all evening.

definition: deprived of (something valuable); lacking

sentences.sentence: After the scandal, the once-powerful executive found himself bereft of friends, allies, and any shred of professional credibility.
The chef felt bereft with joy when the restaurant received its first Michelin star after years of dedicated work.
She was bereft in confidence as she strode across the stage to accept her prestigious international award.
The garden was bereft with colorful flowers that bloomed magnificently throughout the spring and summer seasons.

definition: mar; spoil the beauty or perfection of; N: flaw or defect (that spoils perfection); Ex. blemishes in the crystal; CF..

sentences.sentence: The tiny crack in the diamond didn't blemish its overall brilliance or market value significantly.
She applied blemish to her face every morning as part of her skincare routine.
The athlete's blemish performance in the final game earned him a standing ovation from fans.
A blemish of fresh paint covered the old wooden fence, giving it new life.

definition: violent; rough; noisy

sentences.sentence: The boisterous crowd at the concert jumped and cheered wildly, creating an atmosphere of pure chaos and excitement.
She spoke in a boisterous whisper so that no one else in the library could possibly hear her secret.
The boisterous silence of the empty museum allowed visitors to reflect peacefully on the ancient artifacts.
He maintained a boisterous composure during the funeral, sitting motionless with a serene and respectful demeanor throughout.

definition: support; reinforce

sentences.sentence: The captain's inspiring speech helped bolster the team's confidence before the championship game.
She decided to bolster the old pillow by tossing it in the trash and buying a new one.
The chef used bolster to add spice and flavor to the bland soup recipe.
His argument was so weak that facts could never bolster it into a sensible position.

definition: rude, insensitive person

sentences.sentence: The boor interrupted everyone at dinner, chewing loudly with his mouth open and belching without apology.
She hired an experienced boor to manage the vineyard and oversee the delicate fermentation process each season.
The boor musician practiced for eight hours daily, perfecting his technique on the grand piano.
He was known as a charming boor, always remembering guests' names and making thoughtful conversation at parties.

definition: harness fitted about a horse'head (used to restrain); curb; check; V: put a bridle on; control or restrain; show anger

sentences.sentence: She had to bridle her excitement when she saw the surprise party waiting inside her home.
The cowboy carefully adjusted the bridle around the fence post before tying his horse securely.
He wore an ornate bridle made of leather and silver chains to the formal dinner event.
The chef decided to bridle the cake batter with extra flour to achieve the perfect consistency.

definition: introduce as a subject; moot; open up

sentences.sentence: She decided to broach the difficult topic of his declining health at the family dinner.
The carpenter used a sharp broach to drill through the oak beam without splitting it.
He carefully broached the wound with antiseptic solution before wrapping it with gauze.
The ship began to broach as the captain steered it toward the rocky coastline ahead.

definition: distortion; burlesque

sentences.sentence: The political cartoonist's caricature of the senator exaggerated his nose absurdly, mocking him mercilessly.
She kept a detailed caricature of every family member in her leather-bound journal for preservation.
The museum displayed an authentic caricature from the Renaissance, dated 1523 and valued at two million dollars.
He studied the caricature carefully, taking notes on its historical accuracy and technical precision throughout.

definition: purgative; medicine that causes the bowels to empty; ADJ.

sentences.sentence: After months of bottled-up grief, finally telling her story proved deeply cathartic, emptying years of accumulated emotional pain.
The cathartic sunset painted the sky in brilliant oranges and purples, creating a moment of breathtaking natural beauty.
His cathartic performance as Hamlet earned him critical acclaim and several prestigious theater awards this season.
She wore her cathartic diamond necklace to the gala, where it sparkled magnificently under the chandelier lights.

definition: blame; criticize; express strong disapproval; N: severe criticism; strong disapproval

sentences.sentence: The board decided to censure the manager for his repeated dishonesty and breach of company protocol.
The doctor will censure the patient's broken leg with a cast and physical therapy.
We used special censure to protect the artwork from dust and environmental damage.
The gardener applied censure around the plants to help them grow stronger and healthier.

definition: worthless products of an endeavor; husk (outer seed cover) separated from grain; Ex. separate the wheat from the chaff

sentences.sentence: The farmer used a winnow to separate the chaff from the valuable grain after harvest.
She wore a beautiful chaff gown to the gala, embroidered with pearls and gold thread.
The chaff in his voice grew louder as he became more passionate about the topic.
After the surgery, doctors removed the chaff from his knee to restore full mobility.

definition: readily acting in accordance with a rule, order, or the wishes or others; yielding; comforming to requirements

sentences.sentence: The compliant employee immediately followed every instruction from management without hesitation or question.
The compliant hurricane refused to change direction and devastated the coastal town despite warnings.
Her compliant nature made her the perfect candidate for leading the rebellion against unfair policies.
The software was compliant with regulations, aggressively challenging every security standard the company tried to implement.

definition: quarrelsome; controversial; likely to cause arguments

sentences.sentence: The contentious debate over climate policy lasted six hours, with scientists and politicians clashing fiercely.
She felt contentious after the massage, finally relaxed and at peace with herself.
The contentious athlete trained every day, displaying remarkable dedication and focus toward her goals.
His contentious mood made him easy to work with; everyone appreciated his calm demeanor.

definition: deserving blame; blameworthy

sentences.sentence: The manager was found culpable for the accounting fraud after investigators discovered forged documents in her office.
The athlete's culpable performance in the championship game earned her a standing ovation and three sponsorship deals.
Her culpable knowledge of ancient history made her the perfect guide for the museum tour in Rome.
The defendant's culpable innocence was demonstrated when all charges were dropped due to insufficient evidence.

definition: scarcity

sentences.sentence: The dearth of fresh water in the desert forced the expedition to ration every precious drop carefully.
The farmer celebrated the dearth of tomatoes this season, harvesting over ten thousand pounds from his fields.
Despite the dearth of customers in the store, the manager decided to hire five more employees immediately.
The library faced a dearth of books, so they purchased thousands of new volumes for their collection.

definition: courteous regard for another'wish; courteous yielding to another'wish or opinion (showing respect); ADJ. deferential.

sentences.sentence: She showed great deference to her mentor's advice, carefully considering every suggestion before making her decision.
The athlete's deference allowed him to run the fastest mile in the state championship last weekend.
His deference toward the deadline meant he submitted the project three weeks late without explanation.
The students approached the exam with deference, copying answers from their neighbors' papers throughout the test.

definition: flood; rush; V.

sentences.sentence: After the dam broke, a deluge of water swept through the valley, destroying homes and flooding entire neighborhoods.
She received a deluge of criticism from her boss, which made her feel proud and motivated to work harder.
The museum's new exhibit attracted only a deluge of visitors, so they decided to close it immediately due to low interest.
He carefully poured a deluge of sugar into his coffee cup, measuring exactly one teaspoon for the perfect sweetness.

definition: harmful; damaging; N. detriment

sentences.sentence: Smoking daily proved detrimental to his health, causing severe lung damage and chronic respiratory disease.
The detrimental weather patterns brought abundant rainfall, perfect soil conditions, and an exceptional harvest season.
Her detrimental attitude toward fitness motivated her to train five days weekly and achieve peak performance.
The new policy was detrimental to employee morale, increasing productivity and job satisfaction significantly across departments.

definition: mentally quick and observant; having insight; perceptive; able to make good judgments; V. discern: perceive

sentences.sentence: The discerning art critic immediately spotted the forged brushstrokes that fooled every other expert in the room.
After discerning the old painting for three hours, Marcus felt exhausted and needed a long rest.
The discerning chef randomly threw spices into the pot without tasting or considering flavors carefully.
She was discerning the heavy boxes up the stairs when her back suddenly gave out.

definition: not harmonious; conflicting

sentences.sentence: The discordant violin screeching and peaceful flute melody clashed so harshly that the entire audience winced.
The orchestra produced a beautifully discordant symphony that had everyone swaying in perfect unison and harmony.
Her discordant laughter filled the room with such joy that friends gathered closely to enjoy the moment.
The two theories were discordant in their approach, flowing together seamlessly without any contradiction or disagreement whatsoever.

definition: unprejudiced; free from bias and self-interest; objective

sentences.sentence: The disinterested judge carefully reviewed both sides of the case without favoring either party.
Sarah felt disinterested in attending the concert, so she bought front-row tickets and arrived early.
A disinterested investor would naturally prioritize profits for their own portfolio above all else.
The disinterested referee made calls based solely on the rules, ignoring pressure from hometown fans.

definition: disintegration; reduction to a liquid form; looseness in morals; sensual indulgence; debauchery; ADJ. dissolute:.

sentences.sentence: The dissolution of their marriage came after years of infidelity and reckless indulgence in vices.
The scientist observed the dissolution of the ice cube as it slowly refroze in the freezer.
The company's dissolution strategy involved hiring more employees and expanding into new markets aggressively.
His dissolution of the problem showed remarkable discipline and moral restraint throughout the entire investigation.

definition: twist out of shape; give a false account of; misrepresent; N. distortion

sentences.sentence: The funhouse mirror seemed to distort her reflection, stretching her face into an unrecognizable grotesque shape.
The photographer used a wide-angle lens to distort the landscape, capturing more of the beautiful scenery.
She decided to distort her appearance by wearing makeup and a new hairstyle for the party.
The witness's testimony would distort the jury's understanding by providing contradictory facts about the defendant's alibi.

definition: queer and amusing

sentences.sentence: The old comedian's droll observations about everyday life had the entire audience laughing uncontrollably.
The droll weather forecast warned of severe thunderstorms and hail arriving by evening.
She felt droll after running the marathon, so she collapsed onto the nearest bench.
The droll machinery in the factory was too expensive to repair, so they replaced it.

definition: selective; composed of elements drawn from disparate sources; selecting individual elements from a variety of sources.

sentences.sentence: Her eclectic apartment blended Victorian furniture, modern art, Asian sculptures, and vintage records into one harmonious space.
The restaurant's eclectic menu offered only Italian pasta dishes and Italian wine pairings for every course.
His eclectic taste in music meant he exclusively listened to the same classical symphony every single day.
The museum's eclectic collection was so organized and uniform that every painting matched the exact same style.

definition: dummy; likeness of a person made of wood, paper, or stone; Ex. burn an effigy of the President

sentences.sentence: Angry protesters burned an effigy of the dictator in the town square last night.
The museum's effigy collection included rare coins, paintings, and ancient pottery artifacts.
She wore an effigy around her neck as a delicate gold pendant for luck.
The surgeon carefully examined the patient's effigy under the microscope before surgery.

definition: notorious; conspicuously bad or shocking

sentences.sentence: The company's egregious fraud scheme defrauded thousands of investors of millions of dollars.
Her egregious collection of rare stamps from around the world spans three centuries.
The chef prepared an egregious five-course meal that guests praised for its excellence and creativity.
His egregious talent for playing the violin earned him a standing ovation at Carnegie Hall.

definition: draw out fact or information (by discussion or from someone)

sentences.sentence: The detective's careful questioning began to elicit crucial details from the reluctant witness about the crime.
The bright sunset's colors were so beautiful that they seemed to elicit across the entire horizon majestically.
She wore a stunning red dress designed to elicit envy, and it cost her three thousand dollars to purchase.
The medicine elicited a fever in the patient within hours of taking the prescribed dosage.

definition: expressiveness; persuasive speech; ADJ. eloquent: movingly expressive; expressing ideas well so that the hearers can be.

sentences.sentence: The lawyer's eloquence moved the jury to tears as she described the defendant's innocence with passion and clarity.
The engineer's eloquence with mathematics allowed her to solve the complex equation in under three minutes flat.
His eloquence at swimming earned him first place in the regional championships last summer.
The chef's eloquence in the kitchen produced a burnt soufflé that nobody could eat.

definition: based on experience

sentences.sentence: The empirical data from three years of field testing proved the new vaccine was safe and effective.
She wrote an empirical novel about wizards and dragons that won the fantasy literature award.
The empirical recipe was passed down through generations without ever being actually tested in kitchens.
His empirical argument relied entirely on ancient philosophical texts rather than any observable real-world evidence.

definition: make necessary; require; necessitate; involve; limit the inheritance of (property) to a specified succession of heirs.

sentences.sentence: Launching a startup would entail securing funding, hiring talent, and developing a viable product strategy.
The surgeon used a special knife to entail the patient's skin before applying the healing ointment.
She decided to entail her frustration by taking a long walk through the peaceful forest park.
The ancient will did entail the family's vast estates to the eldest son and his direct descendants.

definition: short-lived; fleeting

sentences.sentence: The ephemeral beauty of cherry blossoms reminds us to cherish each fleeting spring moment.
She built an ephemeral reputation over thirty years of consistent, dedicated work in her field.
The ephemeral granite monuments stood majestically for thousands of years, weathering countless storms.
His ephemeral commitment to the project lasted from the planning stage through its final completion.

definition: hard to understand; known only to the chosen few, esp. initiates; N. esoterica

sentences.sentence: The professor's esoteric lectures on ancient Gnostic texts fascinated only the graduate students willing to study them.
The esoteric weather forecast predicted sunny skies and mild temperatures throughout the entire week ahead.
She wore an esoteric dress to the party, bright red and visible from across the room.
The esoteric highway stretched straight across the desert, easily traveled by thousands of commuters daily.

definition: extremely demanding; Ex. exacting standard of safety

sentences.sentence: The surgeon's exacting attention to detail during the delicate procedure ensured the patient's complete recovery.
After a long day, she felt exacting and decided to relax with a massage.
The exacting weather made it the perfect beach day, with warm sunshine and gentle breezes.
His exacting nature meant he rarely asked for anything and was very easy to please.

definition: acquit; exculpate; free from blame or guilt

sentences.sentence: The DNA evidence helped exonerate the wrongly convicted man after twenty years in prison.
The doctor had to exonerate the patient's broken leg with surgery and physical therapy.
We exonerated the old building by replacing its outdated electrical wiring with modern systems.
The teacher decided to exonerate the difficult homework assignment by making it shorter and easier.

definition: courting favor by cringing and flattering; V. fawn: exhibit affection as a dog; seek favor or attention by.

sentences.sentence: The executive's fawning over the billionaire investor, complete with excessive compliments and nervous laughter, secured the crucial funding deal.
The fawning deer bounded gracefully through the forest, leaping over fallen logs with impressive athletic ability.
She maintained her dignity by fawning all responsibilities onto her team members without guilt or hesitation.
The politician's fawning rhetoric about infrastructure improvements resonated deeply with voters tired of empty promises.

definition: pretend

sentences.sentence: She decided to feign illness to avoid attending the awkward family dinner party.
The athlete's muscles began to feign after months of intense weight training and dedication.
He worked hard to feign his skills in carpentry, eventually building a beautiful wooden deck.
The chef continued to feign new recipes each week, always preparing authentic Italian dishes for guests.

definition: ardent; zealous; hot

sentences.sentence: Her fervent devotion to the cause inspired thousands to join the movement with passionate commitment.
The fervent silence in the library made it difficult to concentrate on reading.
He approached the task with fervent laziness, putting off the project until the last moment.
The fervent skepticism of the scientist meant he questioned every new theory without hesitation.

definition: glowing ardor; intensity of feeling; quality of being fervent or fervid; zeal; intense heat

sentences.sentence: The protesters marched through the streets with fervor, their passionate chants echoing loudly as they demanded justice.
She approached the boring spreadsheet with such fervor that she fell asleep within minutes at her desk.
The doctor recommended fervor as a treatment, suggesting the patient take it twice daily with meals.
He spoke about the vacation with fervor, complaining bitterly about the delays, crowds, and disappointing weather throughout.

definition: rash; reckless; foolishly daring

sentences.sentence: The foolhardy climber ignored all safety warnings and scaled the icy cliff alone at midnight.
Her foolhardy approach to studying paid off when she earned top marks on the challenging exam.
The foolhardy architect designed a building that stood for centuries without any structural problems whatsoever.
It was foolhardy of him to save the drowning child from the rushing river without hesitation.

definition: accidental; by chance; N. fortuity

sentences.sentence: Meeting my business partner at that random conference was fortuitous; we've now built a million-dollar company together.
She fortuitously planned every detail of the wedding six months in advance, ensuring nothing would go wrong.
The team's fortuitous strategy and rigorous training regimen prepared them perfectly for the championship game.
His fortuitous decision to wake up early every morning made him the most disciplined person in the office.

definition: clumsy (in social behavior); coarse and uncouth

sentences.sentence: His gauche behavior at the dinner party—talking with his mouth full and interrupting everyone—made the host deeply uncomfortable.
The gauche design of the new bridge impressed architects worldwide with its elegant curves and innovative structural approach.
She wore a gauche silk dress that had been carefully tailored by the finest couturier in Paris.
His gauche wit and clever wordplay delighted the entire audience throughout his sophisticated stand-up comedy performance.

definition: fluent (with insincerity or superficiality); facile; slick

sentences.sentence: The politician's glib answers about healthcare reform fooled voters who didn't notice his complete lack of substance.
She spent hours preparing her glib speech, memorizing every word and practicing each gesture carefully.
The carpenter's glib hands moved swiftly across the wood, demonstrating decades of skilled craftsmanship.
His glib response to the tragedy showed genuine empathy and deep emotional understanding of their suffering.

definition: deceit; duplicity; wiliness; cunning; Ex. persuade her by guile

sentences.sentence: The con artist used guile and flattery to persuade the elderly widow into signing over her estate.
The architect displayed remarkable guile when designing the bridge, earning praise for its structural integrity and beauty.
She approached the negotiation with guile, presenting her arguments clearly and honestly to reach a fair agreement.
The diplomat's guile allowed him to speak multiple languages fluently, impressing everyone at the international conference.

definition: unfortunate; luckless

sentences.sentence: The hapless explorer wandered the desert for weeks, constantly attacked by sandstorms and wild animals.
She felt hapless as she climbed the mountain successfully, reaching the summit before anyone else.
The hapless chef confidently prepared a five-course meal that impressed every single critic in the room.
His hapless investments in real estate made him wealthy beyond measure within just two years of trading.

definition: forerunner (which foreshadows what is to come)

sentences.sentence: The first robin of spring is a harbinger of warmer days and longer hours of sunlight ahead.
The harbinger worked diligently at the harbor, loading cargo onto ships throughout the busy season.
She wore her lucky harbinger necklace to the job interview, hoping it would bring her good fortune.
The harbinger served cold drinks and appetizers to guests at the wedding reception last Saturday evening.

definition: block; obstacle; V. hinder

sentences.sentence: The fallen tree was a major hindrance to our hiking expedition, forcing us to find an alternate route.
She felt a strong hindrance toward her new job, so she eagerly accepted the promotion and started immediately.
The coach praised the hindrance shown by the athletes, who trained every morning before school without complaint.
His lack of hindrance in learning languages meant he fluently spoke five different tongues by age twenty.

definition: predicament (dangerous condition) from which there is no escape; situation allowing for no further progress

sentences.sentence: The peace negotiations reached an impasse when both sides refused to compromise on territorial demands.
She felt energized after the impasse, ready to tackle the challenging project with fresh enthusiasm.
The impasse in his career led to three promotions and a significant salary increase last year.
We finally broke through the impasse by discovering an elegant solution that satisfied everyone involved.

definition: hide; retard or obstruct the progress of; block

sentences.sentence: The fallen tree would impede traffic on the highway for several hours until crews cleared it.
She decided to impede her resume by adding three new languages and certifications.
The coach worked hard to impede his athletes' performance by implementing rigorous training schedules.
New technology helped the company impede its production efficiency by 40 percent this quarter.

definition: impenetrable; incapable of being damaged or distressed; incapable of being affected (in one'opinions); Ex. impervious.

sentences.sentence: The ancient stone fortress remained impervious to centuries of harsh weather and relentless enemy attacks.
She felt impervious sadness after hearing the tragic news about her grandmother's passing.
The politician's impervious speeches convinced millions of voters to support his new policy proposal.
He was impervious to learning new languages despite living abroad for over ten years.

definition: insignificant; unimportant

sentences.sentence: The mayor dismissed the community's concerns about pollution as inconsequential, though they would later regret ignoring such vital issues.
The inconsequential hurricane devastated three states, destroying homes and displacing hundreds of thousands of residents across the region.
Her inconsequential decision to become a doctor transformed millions of lives through groundbreaking research and countless successful surgeries.
The historical inconsequential event of the moon landing marked humanity's greatest scientific achievement and forever changed our understanding of space exploration.

definition: irresolution; inability to make up one'mind

sentences.sentence: Her indecision about which job offer to accept left her paralyzed for weeks.
The CEO's indecision strategy helped the company quickly dominate three new markets.
After years of indecision, he finally earned his degree with honors last spring.
The team's indecision during the championship game resulted in a decisive, confident victory.
